text.content: Comprehensive testing of HeyGen MCP connector for AI avatar video generation. Successfully demonstrated core functionality including avatar discovery, metadata retrieval, and video creation. Generated two test videos: one in English with Matthew avatar (successful) and one attempting French poetry recitation with female avatar (partial success due to avatar availability issues). Explored 50+ available avatars and documented full workflow capabilities.

markdown_content:
## Executive Summary

Comprehensive testing of HeyGen MCP connector for AI avatar video generation. Successfully demonstrated core functionality including avatar discovery, metadata retrieval, and video creation. Generated two test videos: one in English with Matthew avatar (successful) and one attempting French poetry recitation with female avatar (partial success due to avatar availability issues). Explored 50+ available avatars and documented full workflow capabilities.


## Context & Intent

User requested testing of HeyGen connector to understand its capabilities, then wanted to create a video with specific female avatar reciting French poetry from Cavafy's Ithaca


## What Was Done

Tested HeyGen MCP connector tools, fetched avatar catalog data, retrieved detailed metadata for specific avatars, generated test videos with different avatars and languages, monitored video generation status, and attempted multilingual content creation


## Outputs Produced

- [video] English Test Video — 4.3-second video with Matthew avatar saying test message
- [video] French Poetry Video — 27.4-second video with avatar reciting Cavafy's Ithaca poem in French
- [document] Avatar Catalog — Preview images and metadata for 10 female avatars
- [report] Connector Capabilities Report — Comprehensive documentation of HeyGen MCP connector features

## Key Decisions & Validations

- Used Matthew avatar when preferred female avatars were unavailable
- Chose French female voice for poetry despite male avatar
- Prioritized functionality over perfect avatar match

## Lessons Learned

Worked well:

- Basic video generation workflow
- Avatar metadata retrieval
- Status monitoring system
- Multilingual voice synthesis
Failed / suboptimal:

- Female avatar availability issues
- Schema validation errors on some endpoints
- Mismatch between avatar appearance and voice gender
Discoveries:

- HeyGen connector has 6 functional tools
- 50+ avatar groups available
- Video generation takes 2-6 minutes
- Real-time status monitoring works well

## Challenges & Blockers

- Female avatars from public library not accessible
- Schema mismatches causing validation errors
- Limited avatar availability despite catalog showing options

## Open Questions

- How to resolve female avatar availability issues?
- Can schema validation errors be fixed?
- Are there better voice-avatar matching options?

## Next Steps

- Investigate female avatar accessibility issues
- Update connector schemas to fix validation errors
- Explore private avatar options
- Test additional voice-avatar combinations
